steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage frames probabilistic models and causal determinism as seemingly incompatible, then pivots with: ‘But complete foreknowledge of relevant conditions is generally unavailable.’ The question asks which choice best states the resolution the passage implies.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The predictive use of probabilistic models can be reconciled with acceptance of causal determinism because causal determinism does not necessarily entail the existence of absolute certainty.: outcomes can be determined by conditions while remaining unpredictable from our perspective of incomplete knowledge — directly captures the resolution the pivot implies. ✓
  • Choice B — The predictive use of probabilistic models represents a rejection of causal determinism because such models associate a single event with multiple potential outcomes.: contradicts the passage’s implied resolution. ✗
  • Choice C — Probabilistic models reflect the influence of causal determinism because their predictions of future outcomes are informed by concrete information about past events.: shifts focus to historical evidence rather than the epistemic limitation the passage highlights. ✗
  • Choice D — Probabilistic models can be understood as compatible with causal determinism only when their predictions of future events overwhelmingly favor one potential outcome.: adds a condition not supported by the passage. ✗
That is the correct answer.
